title: Reasons for determination — July 3, 2026
summary: Canada's International Trade Tribunal ruled that cheap imported steel pipe harms domestic producers, triggering new duties to protect Canadian manufacturers from unfair pricing.

title: Finding — June 26, 2026
summary: Canadian companies exporting steel products to the U.S. now face new anti-dumping duties after the CITT confirmed that imported steel was being sold below cost, increasing competition costs for domestic producers.

title: Reasons for determination — June 24, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled on June 24, 2026, that dumped or subsidized imports are injuring Canadian manufacturers, likely triggering tariffs to protect domestic producers from unfair foreign competition.

title: Determination — June 18, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of frozen strawberries from the U.S. now face a 15% anti-dumping duty after CITT found unfair pricing harmed domestic producers.

title: Determination — June 9, 2026
summary: Canada's import tariff on steel fasteners from certain countries will increase by 15% effective immediately, raising costs for manufacturers who rely on these components.

title: Reasons for determination — June 1, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of aluminum products must now pay higher tariffs after the tribunal found domestic producers were injured by cheap imports.

title: Public hearing — May 25, 2026
summary: Canadian businesses importing steel products should monitor this May 2026 CITT hearing outcome, as it may result in new tariff rules or trade remedies affecting their supply costs.

title: Determination — May 15, 2026
summary:
# Trade Intelligence Summary

Canadian manufacturers can now import steel wire rod from eight additional countries without facing anti-dumping duties, expanding sourcing options and potentially reducing input costs.
